batching_primitive.name: HIGH_WATER_LIMIT (64KB body-buffer cap)
batching_primitive.notes: Bounds per-cycle body size; does not aggregate Data events into one receive() message.

servers.name: granian
servers.parser: Rust hyper internals exposed to Python
servers.notes: Rust core but the Rust->Python bridge adds per-chunk cost; ends up slower than pure-Python uvicorn-h11.

top_hot_functions.notes: one syscall per chunk; 73% of cumtime in syscall+bufio.fill loop

prior_cve: CVE-2023-39326 chunk-extension cost cap (does not mitigate raw chunk count)

measurements.notes: indistinguishable from net/http: uses the stdlib decoder
1 syscall for entire body; confirms per-chunk poll loop is the cost

batching_primitive.name: BodyExt::limit_chunks(N) (application-side cap, not aggregator)
batching_primitive.notes: Application sets per-request chunk cap; doesn't aggregate into bigger messages.

prior_issue: hyper#4008 'Provide built-in chunked request limits & CPU-safe streaming helpers' (closed not_planned 2026-01-12)

measurements.notes: single core saturated for full duration
Mode B linear at pacing floor; per-chunk WORK is 5-7us

top_hot_functions.notes: nextTick per chunk -> queue drain becomes superlinear

prior_cve: CVE-2026-7790 cowlib chunked-size (different mechanism, same family)

batching_primitive.name: Netty HttpObjectAggregator
batching_primitive.notes: Available but Vert.x deliberately does NOT install it on the default codec pipeline (would break streaming request().handler() contract).

top_hot_functions.notes: Servlet thread-per-request model adds per-chunk context switch on top of the parser cost

measurements.notes: wall is gap-bounded; CPU* wall gives per-chunk cost

batching_primitive.name: :cowboy_req.read_body(req, %{length: N, period: T})
batching_primitive.notes: Handler-side accumulator: drain up to N bytes per call, batching the per-chunk decoder yields. Does not eliminate the per-chunk decoder cost upstream.

batching_primitive.name: Plug.Conn.read_body(conn, length: N, read_length: N)
batching_primitive.notes: Handler-side coalescing; per-chunk decoder cost above the Plug boundary is unchanged.

batching_primitive.name: Plug.Conn.read_body(conn, length: N, read_length: N)
batching_primitive.notes: Handler-side coalescing does NOT prevent the iolist_size O(N) walk on each chunk inside Bandit's HTTP/1 decoder; the quadratic is upstream of the Plug boundary.

servers.name: nginx (openresty distribution)
servers.runtime: C (openresty wraps stock nginx + LuaJIT body sink)

top_hot_functions.notes: 26.6K calls for 100K chunks (~3.7 chunks per recv)

batching_primitive.name: client_body_buffer_size + proxy_request_buffering on (reverse-proxy mode)
batching_primitive.notes: In reverse-proxy mode (the canonical deployment), nginx fully buffers the decoded request body into client_body_buffer_size (default 8-16 KB; spills to file beyond) BEFORE opening the upstream connection. Upstream sees one Content-Length: request, one TCP send. Per-chunk decode cost is paid by nginx, not the upstream. proxy_request_buffering off re-exposes the upstream.

prior_cve: CVE-2013-2028 ngx_http_parse_chunked stack buffer overflow (correctness, not amp)
scoop.notes: No public discussion of per-chunk-count HTTP/1 CPU amplification against the chunked decoder; client_body_timeout is by-byte, no per-chunk knob.

measurements.notes: First trial ConnRst on cold start; second trial ~5.2s

batching_primitive.name: mod_proxy_http ProxyIOBufferSize (reverse-proxy mode)
batching_primitive.notes: When deployed as reverse proxy, httpd reads the full client body into brigades before forwarding. Default ProxyIOBufferSize 8192.

scoop.notes: LimitRequestBody only; no LimitRequestChunks. Default MPM is event.

servers.name: HAProxy (with Lua applet for body sink)

top_hot_functions.notes: 94K calls for 100K chunks (~0.94/chunk; least coalescing of the three)

batching_primitive.name: option http-buffer-request + tune.bufsize
batching_primitive.notes: Streaming by default; http-buffer-request waits for one full tune.bufsize (16 KB default) before invoking the rule. Not a per-chunk accumulator.

scoop.notes: No chunks-per-second cap; timeout http-request is by total request time only.

servers.name: Kestrel

batching_primitive.name: System.IO.Pipelines + Http1ChunkedEncodingMessageBody.Read() multi-chunk loop
batching_primitive.notes: PumpAsync reads the entire socket-readable buffer into Pipelines, then Read() loops through ParseChunkedPrefix/ReadChunkedData states for every chunk present in the buffer before calling FlushAsync once and AdvanceTo. Per-chunk wake-up of the application handler is avoided; the handler sees one Pipe ReadResult containing N coalesced chunks (the data bytes only; chunk-extension and CRLF metadata are stripped in the pump).

profile_top_functions: System.Threading.LowLevelLifoSemaphore.WaitForSignal (idle waits, 88.7% of busy thread cumulative, expected for sampled trace)

scoop.title: Kestrel HTTP request smuggling via chunk-extension parser (lone \n)

scoop.relevance: response-side chunking issues; not request-side amplification

scoop.observation: No public discussion of per-chunk amortised-CPU amplification against Kestrel found; Kestrel's PumpAsync/Pipelines design appears to have closed the class structurally before it was named.

servers.name: waitress

measurements.note: dominated by 100us pacing floor; intermittent 250K connection-reset under sustained pacing

servers.scaling_note: Super-linear 50K->250K (17x time for 5x N). Likely OverflowableBuffer overflow-to-tempfile threshold at inbuf_overflow=524288 bytes.

servers.name: tornado
servers.buffer_location: per-Data-event data_received callbacks; default _BodyReader joins on chunk-zero
servers.scaling_note: Super-linear (50K=0.26s, 250K=4.59s, 17x for 5x N). Per-Data-event callback chain (_try_inline_read + read_bytes futures) dominates.

batching_primitive.name: Reactor->Tempfile body buffer; handler gets fully-rewindable IO
batching_primitive.notes: Batches at the handler boundary (one Rack call per request, complete Tempfile body). Does NOT batch per-chunk decode cost; reactor still loops decode_chunk in pure Ruby.

prior_cve: CVE-2024-21647 / GHSA-c2f4-cvqm-65w2 (chunk-extension size cap, fixed 6.4.2/5.6.8; does not cap raw chunk count)

servers.name: unicorn

measurements.notes: RTT-noise floor; bridge fits body in 1-2 recvs

batching_primitive.name: TeeInput memory-then-spill body buffer
batching_primitive.notes: client_body_buffer_size defaults to 112KB in-memory; spills to Tempfile beyond. Handler sees fully-buffered StreamInput. Ragel C parser keeps per-chunk decode cheap.

scoop.notes: No published CVE for chunked-TE amplification on the body framing path; smuggling-class issues predate the 2021 ragel hardening.

servers.notes: Initial Mode B 250K reset was wave-1 asgi-net contamination (Apache container shared `server` alias). Clean network gave 26.5s wall.

scoop.notes: No advisory for per-chunk amplification on async-http / protocol-http1.
